rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO MENDOZA LUNA VS. BEHROUZ HALALI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Treble Damages Awarded By Jury Under San Francisco Rent Ordinance Claims
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 6, 2026, line 2. PLAINTIFF RODRIGO LUNA'S Motion To Treble Damages Awarded By Jury Under San Francisco Rent Ordinance Claims. Plaintiff's motion to treble damages against defendants Behrouz Halali and Behnam Halali is GRANTED. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO MENDOZA LUNA VS. BEHROUZ HALALI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party After Jury Trial
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 6, 2026, line 2. PLAINTIFF RODRIGO LUNA'S Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party After Jury Trial. Plaintiff's motion for attorney's fees is GRANTED in part. The court determines that plaintiff is the prevailing party as he prevailed on his rent ordinance claims. Those claims also entitle him to reasonable attorney's fees. (San Francisco Administrative Code section 37.9(f).) The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PORTER VS. ZACKS & FREEDMAN, PC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 6, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANT ZACKS & FREEDMAN, P.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Zacks & Freedman, PC moves for summary judgment, or in the alternative summary adjudication, on all of Plaintiff Sandra Porter's claims arising under the Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA). Defendant's motion is premised primarily on the assertion that Plaintiff was not a "qualified individual" as a matter of law and that Defendant lacked notice of Plaintiff's disability or need for accommodation. Defendant further contends that Plaintiff's receipt of state disability benefits judicially estops her from asserting FEHA claims. The court denies the motion for the reasons stated herein. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ROBINSON ET AL VS. JOHN EBERLING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: =Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Tony Benn, Angela Kellum, Deshay Dorhan, And Angelique Mayweather, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 6, 2026, line 7. DEFENDANTS TENANTS AND OWNERS DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, JOHN STEWART COMPANY, HOTEL GRAND SOUTHERN'S Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Tony Benn, Angela Kellum, Deshay Dorhan, And Angelique Mayweather, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Hearing required. Plaintiffs' counsel should be prepared at the hearing to provide authority for the request to hear the basis for the opposition in camera. Ordinarily, the court may not decide a motion based on information that is not known to both sides. One exception is when counsel seeks to withdraw from representation, but that is a different matter because the opposing party does not have standing to object to a change in counsel or a withdrawal of representation. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. VICTOR A GONZALEZ SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 6, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for a deemed admitted order on the Requests for Admission it served on defendant Victor A. Gonzalez, Sr., attached as Exhibit A to the Agne Declaration, is granted. Gonzalez has not responded, making a deemed-admitted order mandatory. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) Sanctions are also mandatory. (Id.) The court orders Gonzalez to pay a nominal sanction of $15 to Wells Fargo within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
